title: incorrect or unclear error message: return data type does not match out-of-block declaration data-type ... Prototype data type: 'LOGIC_IMPLICIT' ... Declaration data type: 'logic'
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

Can you please attach an example that shows the issue or missing feature?  (Must be openly licensed, completely self-contained so can directly run what you provide. Ideally use test_regress format, see https://veripool.org/guide/latest/contributing.html?highlight=test_regress#reporting-bugs)

```
git diff
diff --git a/test_regress/t/t_class_extern.v b/test_regress/t/t_class_extern.v
index 7310da8ea..30b3cb78b 100644
--- a/test_regress/t/t_class_extern.v
+++ b/test_regress/t/t_class_extern.v
@@ -8,7 +8,7 @@ class Cls;
    int value;
    extern function int ext_f_np;
    extern function int ext_f_p();
-   extern function int ext_f_i(int in);
+   extern function ext_f_i(int in);
    extern static function int get_1();
    extern task ext_t_np;
    extern task ext_t_p();
@@ -33,8 +33,7 @@ function int Cls::ext_f_p();
    return value;
 endfunction
 
-function int Cls::ext_f_i(int in);
-   return in+1;
+function Cls::ext_f_i(int in);
 endfunction
 
 function int Cls::get_1();
```

What output from that test indicates it is wrong, and what is the correct or expected output?  (Or, please make test self-checking if possible.)

```
%Error-PROTOTYPEMIS: t/t_class_extern.v:11:20: In prototype for 'ext_f_i', return data type does not match out-of-block declaration data-type (IEEE 1800-2023 8.24)
                                             : ... note: In instance 't'
                                             : ... Prototype data type: 'LOGIC_IMPLICIT'
                                             : ... Declaration data type: 'logic'
   11 |    extern function ext_f_i(int in);
      |                    ^~~~~~~
                     ~/src/verilator/test_regress/../include/verilated_std.sv:55:19: ... Location of out-of-block declaration
   55 |       if (m_bound == 0 || num() < m_bound) begin
      |                   ^~
                     ... For error description see https://verilator.org/warn/PROTOTYPEMIS?v=5.041
%Warning-WIDTHEXPAND: t/t_class_extern.v:89:25: Operator NEQ expects 32 or 4 bits on the LHS, but LHS's METHODCALL 'ext_f_i' generates 1 bits.
                                              : ... note: In instance 't'
   89 |       if (c.ext_f_i(10) != 11) $stop;
      |                         ^~
                      ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/WIDTHEXPAND?v=5.041
                      ... Use "/* verilator lint_off WIDTHEXPAND */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s), 1 warning(s)
```

Not clear what `Prototype data type: 'LOGIC_IMPLICIT'` means.

In both cases there is no type and implicit type should be the same.

Not clear what this means:

```
                     ~/src/verilator/test_regress/../include/verilated_std.sv:55:19: ... Location of out-of-block declaration
   55 |       if (m_bound == 0 || num() < m_bound) begin
      |                   ^~
```

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?  Did you try it with other simulators?

```
Verilator 5.041 devel rev v5.040-186-g6f250b3f8
```

What OS and distribution are you using?

```
Linux 4.18.0-553.22.1.el8_10.x86_64
```

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

(Please avoid attaching screenshots that show text - you can convert images to text using e.g. https://ocr.space)
